revision.text:
'''住吉 綱主'''（すみのえ の つなぬし、[[天平]]元年（[[729年]]） - [[延暦]]24年[[2月10日 (旧暦)|2月10日]]（[[805年]][[3月14日]]）は、[[奈良時代]]から[[平安時代]]初期にかけての[[貴族]]。[[氏姓]]は池原公のち住吉朝臣。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[近衛少将]]。
== 経歴 == [[近衛府|近衛将監]]在官中の[[延暦]]8年（[[789年]]）[[下総国#国司|下総大掾]]を兼ね（この時の位階は[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位上]]）<ref>『[[続日本紀]]』延暦8年2月4日条</ref>、翌延暦9年（[[790年]]）従五位下に叙せられる<ref>『続日本紀』延暦9年2月26日条</ref>。延暦10年（[[791年]]）[[常陸国#国司|常陸大掾]]を兼任する<ref>『続日本紀』延暦10年正月22日条</ref>。同年[[池原氏]]・[[上毛野氏]]の両氏は[[豊城入彦命]]の子孫であること、またその子孫は東国で6系統に分かれ居住地に因んで[[賜姓]]を受けていることから、居住地の[[摂津国]][[住吉郡]]<ref>現在の[[大阪市]][[住吉区]]および[[住之江区]]。</ref>に因んで[[住吉氏|住吉朝臣]]の賜姓を申請し、兄弟1名とともにこれを許されている<ref>『続日本紀』延暦10年4月5日条</ref>。
その後、延暦14年（[[795年]]）従五位上<ref>『[[日本後紀]]』延暦14年10月28日条</ref>、延暦18年（[[799年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]と昇進し<ref>『日本後紀』延暦18年5月8日条</ref>、[[従四位|従四位下]]・近衛少将に至る<ref name="a">『日本後紀』延暦24年2月10日条</ref>。 延暦24年（[[805年]]）2月10日[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]。[[享年]]77。最終[[官位]]は[[散位]]従四位下<ref name="a" />。
== 人物 == [[弓術]]が得意であったことから[[近衛府]]の[[官人]]となり、近衛将曹・近衛将監を歴任した。公務に精勤し、宿衛を怠ることが無く、近衛府の士卒の信頼を得て近衛少将にまで昇った。[[鷹狩|鷹犬]]を愛好したという。<ref name="a" />
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1995年 *[[森田悌]]『日本後紀 （上）』講談社学術文庫、2006年
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみのえ の つなぬし}} [[Category:奈良時代の人物]] [[Category:平安時代の人物]] [[Category:729年生]] [[Category:805年生]]

'''住吉 綱主'''（すみのえ の つなぬし、[[天平]]元年（[[729年]]） - [[延暦]]24年[[2月10日 (旧暦)|2月10日]]（[[805年]][[3月14日]]）は、[[奈良時代]]から[[平安時代]]初期にかけての[[貴族]]。[[氏姓]]は池原公のち住吉朝臣。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[近衛少将]]。
== 経歴 == [[近衛府|近衛将監]]在官中の[[延暦]]8年（[[789年]]）[[下総国#国司|下総大掾]]を兼ね（この時の位階は[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位上]]）<ref>『[[続日本紀]]』延暦8年2月4日条</ref>、翌延暦9年（[[790年]]）従五位下に叙せられる<ref>『続日本紀』延暦9年2月26日条</ref>。延暦10年（[[791年]]）[[常陸国#国司|常陸大掾]]を兼任する<ref>『続日本紀』延暦10年正月22日条</ref>。同年[[池原氏]]・[[上毛野氏]]の両氏は[[豊城入彦命]]の子孫であること、またその子孫は東国で6系統に分かれ居住地に因んで[[賜姓]]を受けていることから、居住地の[[摂津国]][[住吉郡]]<ref>現在の[[大阪市]][[住吉区]]および[[住之江区]]。</ref>に因んで[[住吉氏|住吉朝臣]]の賜姓を申請し、兄弟1名とともにこれを許されている<ref>『続日本紀』延暦10年4月5日条</ref>。
その後、延暦14年（[[795年]]）従五位上<ref>『[[日本後紀]]』延暦14年10月28日条</ref>、延暦18年（[[799年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]と昇進し<ref>『日本後紀』延暦18年5月8日条</ref>、[[従四位|従四位下]]・近衛少将に至る<ref name="a">『日本後紀』延暦24年2月10日条</ref>。 延暦24年（[[805年]]）2月10日[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]。[[享年]]77。最終[[官位]]は[[散位]]従四位下<ref name="a" />。
== 人物 == [[弓術]]が得意であったことから[[近衛府]]の[[官人]]となり、近衛将曹・近衛将監を歴任した。公務に精勤し、宿衛を怠ることが無く、近衛府の士卒の信頼を得て近衛少将にまで昇った。[[鷹狩|鷹犬]]を愛好したという。<ref name="a" />
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *宇治谷孟『続日本紀 （下）』[[講談社学術文庫]]、1995年 *[[森田悌]]『日本後紀 （上）』講談社学術文庫、2006年
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみのえ の つなぬし}} [[Category:奈良時代の人物]] [[Category:平安時代の人物]] [[Category:729年生]] [[Category:805年生]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]]

その後、延暦14年（[[795年]]）従五位上<ref>『[[日本後紀]]』延暦14年10月28日条</ref>、延暦18年（[[799年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]と昇進し<ref>『日本後紀』延暦18年5月8日条</ref>、[[従四位|従四位下]]・近衛少将に至る<ref name="a">『日本後紀』延暦24年2月10日条</ref>。 延暦24年（[[805年]]）2月10日[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]。[[享年]]77。最終[[官位]]は[[散位]]従四位下<ref name="a" />。
== 人物 == [[弓術]]が得意であったことから[[近衛府]]の[[官人]]となり、近衛将曹・近衛将監を歴任した。公務に精勤し、宿衛を怠ることが無く、近衛府の士卒の信頼を得て近衛少将にまで昇った。[[鷹狩|鷹犬]]を愛好したという。<ref name="a" />

revision.comment: 官歴セクション追加、経歴セクション推敲。
revision.text:
'''住吉 綱主'''（すみのえ の つなぬし、[[天平]]元年（[[729年]]） - [[延暦]]24年[[2月10日 (旧暦)|2月10日]]（[[805年]][[3月14日]]）は、[[奈良時代]]から[[平安時代]]初期にかけての[[貴族]]。[[氏姓]]は[[池原氏|池原公]]のち[[住吉氏|住吉朝臣]]。名は'''縄主'''とも記される。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[近衛少将]]。
== 経歴 == [[延暦]]4年（[[785年]]）[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]・[[近衛府|近衛将監]]に叙任され、同年11月には外従五位上と続けて昇叙される。その後長く近衛将監を務める傍らで、[[下総国#国司|下総大掾]]・[[常陸国#国司|常陸大掾]]と地方官を兼ね、延暦9年（[[790年]]）には[[内位]]の従五位下に叙せられている。延暦10年（[[791年]]）[[池原氏]]・[[上毛野氏]]の両氏は[[豊城入彦命]]の子孫であること、またその子孫は東国で6系統に分かれ居住地に因んで[[賜姓]]を受けていることから、居住地の[[摂津国]][[住吉郡]]（現在の[[大阪市]][[住吉区]]および[[住之江区]]）に因んで[[住吉氏|住吉朝臣]]の賜姓を申請し、兄弟1名とともにこれを許されている。
その後、延暦14年（[[795年]]）従五位上、延暦18年（[[799年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]と昇進し、[[従四位|従四位下]]・近衛少将に至る<ref name="a">『日本後紀』延暦24年2月10日条</ref>。 延暦24年（[[805年]]）2月10日[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]。[[享年]]77。最終[[官位]]は[[散位]]従四位下<ref name="a" />。
== 人物 == [[弓術]]が得意であったことから[[近衛府]]の[[官人]]となり、近衛将曹・近衛将監を歴任した。公務に精勤し、宿衛を怠ることが無く、近衛府の士卒の信頼を得て近衛少将にまで昇った。[[鷹狩|鷹犬]]を愛好したという。<ref name="a" />
== 官歴 == 『[[六国史]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[近衛府|近衛将曹]]。[[正六位|正六位上]] *[[延暦]]4年（[[785年]]） 9月8日：[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]。10月12日：近衛将監。11月25日：外従五位上 *延暦8年（[[789年]]） 2月4日：兼[[下総国#国司|下総大掾]] *延暦9年（[[790年]]） 2月26日：従五位下（[[内位]]） *延暦10年（[[791年]]） 正月22日：兼[[常陸国#国司|常陸大掾]]。4月5日：[[池原氏|池原公]]から[[住吉氏|住吉朝臣]]に[[改姓]] *延暦14年（[[795年]]） 10月28日：従五位上 *延暦18年（[[799年]]） 5月8日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *時期不詳：[[従四位|従四位下]]。近衛少将
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （下）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1995年 *[[森田悌]]『日本後紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、2006年
{{DEFAULTSORT:すみのえ の つなぬし}} [[Category:奈良時代の貴族]] [[Category:平安時代初期の貴族]] [[Category:日本古代の武人・武官]] [[Category:729年生]] [[Category:805年生]]

'''住吉 綱主'''（すみのえ の つなぬし）は、[[奈良時代]]から[[平安時代]]初期にかけての[[貴族]]。[[氏姓制度|氏姓]]は[[池原氏|池原]]公のち住吉[[朝臣]]。名は'''縄主'''とも記される。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[近衛府|近衛少将]]。
== 経歴 == [[延暦]]4年（[[785年]]）[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]・[[近衛府|近衛将監]]に叙任され、同年11月には外従五位上と続けて昇叙される。その後長く近衛将監を務める傍らで、[[下総国#国司|下総大掾]]・[[常陸国司|常陸大掾]]と地方官を兼ね、延暦9年（[[790年]]）には[[内位]]の従五位下に叙せられている。延暦10年（[[791年]]）[[池原氏]]・[[上毛野氏]]の両氏は[[豊城入彦命]]の子孫であること、またその子孫は東国で6系統に分かれ居住地に因んで賜姓を受けていることから、居住地の[[摂津国]][[住吉郡]]（現在の[[大阪市]][[住吉区]]および[[住之江区]]）に因んで住吉朝臣の賜姓を申請し、兄弟1名と共にこれを許されている。
その後、延暦14年（[[795年]]）従五位上、延暦18年（[[799年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]と昇進し、従四位下・近衛少将に至る<ref name="a">『日本後紀』延暦24年2月10日条</ref>。 延暦24年（[[805年]]）2月10日卒去。享年77。最終官位は[[散位]]従四位下<ref name="a" />。
== 人物 == [[弓術]]が得意であったことから[[近衛府]]の[[官人]]となり、近衛将曹・近衛将監を歴任した。公務に精勤し、宿衛を怠ることが無く、近衛府の士卒の信頼を得て近衛少将にまで昇った。[[鷹狩|鷹犬]]を愛好したという。<ref name="a" />
== 官歴 == 『[[六国史]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[近衛府|近衛将曹]]。[[正六位|正六位上]] *[[延暦]]4年（[[785年]]） 9月8日：[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]。10月12日：近衛将監。11月25日：外従五位上 *延暦8年（[[789年]]） 2月4日：兼[[下総国#国司|下総大掾]] *延暦9年（[[790年]]） 2月26日：従五位下（[[内位]]） *延暦10年（[[791年]]） 正月22日：兼[[常陸国司|常陸大掾]]。4月5日：池原公から朝臣に改姓 *延暦14年（[[795年]]） 10月28日：従五位上 *延暦18年（[[799年]]） 5月8日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *時期不詳：[[従四位|従四位下]]。近衛少将
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （下）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1995年 *[[森田悌]]『日本後紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、2006年

{{基礎情報 公家 | 氏名 = 住吉綱主 | 画像 = | 画像サイズ = | 画像説明 = | 時代 = [[奈良時代]] - [[平安時代]]初期 | 生誕 = [[天平]]元年（[[729年]]） | 死没 = [[延暦]]24年[[2月10日 (旧暦)|2月10日]]（[[805年]][[3月14日]]） | 改名 = | 別名 = 縄主 | 諡号 = | 神号 = | 戒名 = | 墓所 = | 官位 = [[従四位|従四位下]]・[[近衛府|近衛少将]] | 主君 = [[光仁天皇]]→[[桓武天皇]] | 氏族 = [[池原氏|池原]][[キミ (カバネ)|公]]→住吉[[朝臣]] | 父母 = | 兄弟 = | 妻 = | 子 = | 特記事項 = }}
'''住吉 綱主'''（すみのえ の つなぬし）は、[[奈良時代]]から[[平安時代]]初期にかけての[[貴族]]。[[カバネ|氏姓]]は[[池原氏|池原]][[キミ (カバネ)|公]]のち住吉[[朝臣]]。名は'''縄主'''とも記される。[[官位]]は[[従四位|従四位下]]・[[近衛府|近衛少将]]。
== 経歴 == [[延暦]]4年（[[785年]]）[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]・[[近衛府|近衛将監]]に叙任され、同年11月には外従五位上と続けて昇叙される。その後長く近衛将監を務める傍らで、[[下総国#国司|下総大掾]]・[[常陸国司|常陸大掾]]と地方官を兼ね、延暦9年（[[790年]]）には[[内位]]の従五位下に叙せられている。延暦10年（[[791年]]）[[池原氏]]・[[上毛野氏]]の両氏は[[豊城入彦命]]の子孫であること、またその子孫は東国で6系統に分かれ居住地に因んで賜姓を受けていることから、居住地の[[摂津国]][[住吉郡]]（現在の[[大阪市]][[住吉区]]および[[住之江区]]）に因んで住吉朝臣の賜姓を申請し、兄弟1名と共にこれを許されている。
その後、延暦14年（[[795年]]）従五位上、延暦18年（[[799年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]と昇進し、従四位下・近衛少将に至る<ref name="a">『日本後紀』延暦24年2月10日条</ref>。 延暦24年（[[805年]]）2月10日卒去。享年77。最終官位は[[散位]]従四位下<ref name="a" />。
== 人物 == [[弓術]]が得意であったことから[[近衛府]]の[[官人]]となり、近衛将曹・近衛将監を歴任した。公務に精勤し、宿衛を怠ることが無く、近衛府の士卒の信頼を得て近衛少将にまで昇った。[[鷹狩|鷹犬]]を愛好したという。<ref name="a" />
== 官歴 == 『[[六国史]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[近衛府|近衛将曹]]。[[正六位|正六位上]] *[[延暦]]4年（[[785年]]） 9月8日：[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]。10月12日：近衛将監。11月25日：外従五位上 *延暦8年（[[789年]]） 2月4日：兼[[下総国#国司|下総大掾]] *延暦9年（[[790年]]） 2月26日：従五位下（[[内位]]） *延暦10年（[[791年]]） 正月22日：兼[[常陸国司|常陸大掾]]。4月5日：池原公から朝臣に改姓 *延暦14年（[[795年]]） 10月28日：従五位上 *延暦18年（[[799年]]） 5月8日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *時期不詳：[[従四位|従四位下]]。近衛少将
== 脚注 == {{Reflist}}
== 参考文献 == *[[宇治谷孟]]『続日本紀 （下）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、1995年 *[[森田悌]]『日本後紀 （上）』[[講談社]]〈[[講談社学術文庫]]〉、2006年

== 経歴 == [[延暦]]4年（[[785年]]）[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]・[[近衛府|近衛将監]]に叙任され、同年11月には外従五位上と続けて昇叙される。その後長く近衛将監を務める傍らで、[[下総国#国司|下総大掾]]・[[常陸国司|常陸大掾]]と地方官を兼ね、延暦9年（[[790年]]）には[[内位]]の従五位下に叙せられている。延暦10年（[[791年]]）[[池原氏]]・[[上毛野氏]]の両氏は[[豊城入彦命]]の子孫であること、またその子孫は東国で6系統に分かれ居住地に因んで賜姓を受けていることから、居住地の[[摂津国]][[住吉郡]]（現在の[[大阪市]][[住吉区]]および[[住之江区]]）に因んで住吉朝臣の賜姓を申請し、兄弟1名と共にこれを許されている。
その後、延暦14年（[[795年]]）従五位上、延暦18年（[[799年]]）[[正五位|正五位下]]と昇進し、[[従四位|従四位下]]・近衛少将に至る。 延暦24年（[[805年]]）2月10日[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]。[[享年]]77。最終[[官位]]は[[散位]]従四位下。

== 官歴 == 『[[六国史]]』による。 *時期不詳：[[近衛府|近衛将曹]]。[[正六位|正六位上]] *[[延暦]]4年（[[785年]]） 9月8日：[[外位|外]][[従五位|従五位下]]。10月12日：近衛将監。11月25日：外従五位上 *延暦8年（[[789年]]） 2月4日：兼[[下総国#国司|下総大掾]] *延暦9年（[[790年]]） 2月26日：従五位下（[[内位]]） *延暦10年（[[791年]]） 正月22日：兼[[常陸国司|常陸大掾]]。4月5日：池原公から朝臣に改姓 *延暦14年（[[795年]]） 10月28日：従五位上 *延暦18年（[[799年]]） 5月8日：[[正五位|正五位下]] *時期不詳：[[従四位|従四位下]]。近衛少将 *延暦24年（[[805年]]） 2月10日：[[崩御#卒去|卒去]]（[[散位]]従四位下）

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the [[Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]].
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the [[Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]].
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the [[Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]].
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the [[Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]].
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the [[Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]].
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the [[Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]].
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.

Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the [[Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]].
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.

'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the [[Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]].
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the [[Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]].
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]] and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the Inner [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade.
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the Outer [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the Inner [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade.
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:MAINSPACE}}--qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the Outer [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the Inner [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade.
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the Outer [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the Inner [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade.
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the Outer [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the Inner [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade.
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the Outer [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the Inner [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade.
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the Outer [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the Inner [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade.
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the Outer [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the Inner [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade.
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

revision.comment: trimming off this bad category
revision.text:
{{Infobox noble | name = Tsunanushi Suminoe | image = | image_size = | caption = | period = [[Nara period]] to early [[Heian period]] | birth_date = [[Tenpyō]] 1 (729) | death_date = [[Enryaku]] 24, [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] ([[805]], [[March 14]]) | alias = Nawanushi | ranks = [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]]; [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]] | clan = [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] → [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]] | parents = | siblings = }}
'''Tsunanushi Suminoe''' (住吉 綱主) was a nobleman of the [[Nara period]] to the early [[Heian period]]. His [[uji-kabane|clan title]] transitioned from [[Ikehara clan|Ikehara no Kimi]] to [[Suminoe clan|Suminoe no Asomi]]. His name is also recorded as '''Nawanushi'''. He attained the rank of [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]] and served as a [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]].
== Career == In [[Enryaku]] 4 (785), Tsunanushi was granted the Outer [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade and appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. Later that year, he was promoted to [[Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]. Serving as a [[Konoe no Shōken|Guard Inspector]], he concurrently held provincial posts, including [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]] and [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. In [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), he achieved the Inner [[Junior Fifth Rank]], Lower Grade.
In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
Promotions followed steadily: in [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), he was elevated to [[Junior Fifth Rank|Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade]]; in [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), to [[Senior Fifth Rank|Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade]]; and eventually to [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]], serving as [[Konoe no Shōshō|Captain of the Imperial Guard]]. He passed away on [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]] of [[Enryaku]] 24 (805), aged 77. His final rank was [[Junior Fourth Rank|Junior Fourth Rank, Lower Grade]].
== Personality and Achievements == Tsunanushi excelled in [[archery]], earning him a position in the [[Konoe|Imperial Guard]]. He worked diligently, serving with dedication and gaining the trust of the Guard soldiers. His love of [[hunting|falconry]] and dogs was noted.<ref name="nihon-koki">*Nihon Kōki*, Enryaku 24, 2nd month, 10th day entry.</ref>
== Appointments == According to the *[[Rikkokushi]]*: * [[Enryaku]] 4 (785): - [[9th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. - [[10th month]] [[12th day (lunar calendar)|12th day]]: Appointed [[Konoe no Shōken|Inspector of the Imperial Guard]]. - [[11th month]] [[25th day (lunar calendar)|25th day]]: Outer Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 8 (789), [[2nd month]] [[4th day (lunar calendar)|4th day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Shimōsa Province|Shimōsa Dajō]]. * [[Enryaku]] 9 (790), [[2nd month]] [[26th day (lunar calendar)|26th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 10 (791): - [[1st month]] [[22nd day (lunar calendar)|22nd day]]: Concurrently appointed [[Hitachi Province|Hitachi Dajō]]. - [[4th month]] [[5th day (lunar calendar)|5th day]]: Name changed to Suminoe no Asomi. * [[Enryaku]] 14 (795), [[10th month]] [[28th day (lunar calendar)|28th day]]: Promoted to Inner Junior Fifth Rank, Upper Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 18 (799), [[5th month]] [[8th day (lunar calendar)|8th day]]: Promoted to Senior Fifth Rank, Lower Grade. * [[Enryaku]] 24 (805): - [[1st month]] [[21st day (lunar calendar)|21st day]]: One person granted an official stipend. - [[2nd month]] [[10th day (lunar calendar)|10th day]]: Passed away.
== References == * [[Ujitani Mitsuru|Mitsuru Ujitani]], ''Zoku Nihon Kōki (Lower Volume)'', [[Kodansha]] Academic Library, 1995. * [[Morita Yasushi|Yasushi Morita]], ''Nihon Kōki (Upper Volume)'', Kodansha Academic Library, 2006.

In [[Enryaku]] 10 (791), Tsunanushi and his brother successfully petitioned for a change in their clan title to reflect their ancestral ties to [[Tōjō Province|Tōjō]] and [[Suminoe District]] (now part of [[Osaka City]]). The family name was changed to Suminoe no Asomi.
